text:
(a) Of each fee collected under Sections 521.421 (License Fees; Examination Fees) (b) and (g) and Sections 522.029 (Fees) (f) and (g), the Department of Public Safety shall send $5 to the comptroller for deposit to the credit of the motorcycle education fund account.

(b) Money deposited to the credit of the motorcycle education fund account may be used only to defray the cost of:

(1) administering the motorcycle operator training and safety program; and

(2) conducting the motorcyclist safety and share the road campaign described by Section 201.621 (Motorcyclist Safety and Share the Road Campaign) .

(c) The comptroller shall report to the governor and legislature not later than the first Monday in November of each even-numbered year on the condition of the account. The report must contain:

(1) a statement of the amount of money deposited to the credit of the account for the year;

(2) a statement of the amount of money disbursed by the comptroller from the account for the year;

(3) a statement of the balance of money in the account;

(4) a list of persons and entities that have received money from the account, including information for each person or entity that shows the amount of money received; and

(5) a statement of any significant problems encountered in administering the account, with recommendations for their solution.

(d) The department may apply for and accept gifts, grants, and donations from any organization to be deposited in the motorcycle education fund account for the purpose of improving motorcycle safety in this state.
markdown:
# Transp. Code
## Title 7
### Chapter 662. Motorcycle Operator Training & Safety
##### § 662.011 — Motorcycle Education Fund Account

(a) Of each fee collected under Sections 521.421 (License Fees; Examination Fees) (b) and (g) and Sections 522.029 (Fees) (f) and (g), the Department of Public Safety shall send $5 to the comptroller for deposit to the credit of the motorcycle education fund account.

(b) Money deposited to the credit of the motorcycle education fund account may be used only to defray the cost of:

    (1) administering the motorcycle operator training and safety program; and

    (2) conducting the motorcyclist safety and share the road campaign described by Section 201.621 (Motorcyclist Safety and Share the Road Campaign) .

(c) The comptroller shall report to the governor and legislature not later than the first Monday in November of each even-numbered year on the condition of the account. The report must contain:

    (1) a statement of the amount of money deposited to the credit of the account for the year;

    (2) a statement of the amount of money disbursed by the comptroller from the account for the year;

    (3) a statement of the balance of money in the account;

    (4) a list of persons and entities that have received money from the account, including information for each person or entity that shows the amount of money received; and

    (5) a statement of any significant problems encountered in administering the account, with recommendations for their solution.

(d) The department may apply for and accept gifts, grants, and donations from any organization to be deposited in the motorcycle education fund account for the purpose of improving motorcycle safety in this state.

*Acts 2023, 88th Leg., R.S., Ch. 270 (S.B.478), Sec. 12, eff. January 1, 2024.*

subsections.label: a
subsections.text: Of each fee collected under Sections 521.421 (License Fees; Examination Fees) (b) and (g) and Sections 522.029 (Fees) (f) and (g), the Department of Public Safety shall send $5 to the comptroller for deposit to the credit of the motorcycle education fund account.

subsections.label: b
subsections.text: Money deposited to the credit of the motorcycle education fund account may be used only to defray the cost of:
administering the motorcycle operator training and safety program; and
conducting the motorcyclist safety and share the road campaign described by Section 201.621 (Motorcyclist Safety and Share the Road Campaign) .

subsections.label: c

a statement of the amount of money deposited to the credit of the account for the year;
a statement of the amount of money disbursed by the comptroller from the account for the year;
a statement of the balance of money in the account;
a list of persons and entities that have received money from the account, including information for each person or entity that shows the amount of money received; and
a statement of any significant problems encountered in administering the account, with recommendations for their solution.

subsections.label: d
subsections.text: The department may apply for and accept gifts, grants, and donations from any organization to be deposited in the motorcycle education fund account for the purpose of improving motorcycle safety in this state.

history.raw: Acts 2023, 88th Leg., R.S., Ch. 270 (S.B.478), Sec. 12, eff. January 1, 2024.
